name: NEPHROS INC
business: Nephros develops and markets high-performance water filtration solutions for points of use, with a core focus on medical-grade water filtration. Its medical filtration portfolio includes infection control and dialysis water products, all FDA 510(k)-cleared as Class II medical devices. The company also offers commercial water filters that improve taste and odor and reduce contaminants including PFAS.
sellsTo: Laboratories, manufacturing facilities, aviation environments, and government buildings

exposures.sentence: Nephros is a commercial-stage company that develops and markets high-performance water filtration solutions for points of use, with a core focus on medical-grade water filtration.

catalysts.event: License and Supply Agreement extension with Medica
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, the Company signed a new agreement with Medica which extends the term until December 31, 2030, unless earlier terminated by either party in accordance with the terms of the License and Supply Agreement.

catalysts.event: Minimum annual purchase commitments from Medica for 2026-2030
catalysts.sentence: In exchange for the rights granted, the Company has agreed to make minimum annual aggregate purchases from Medica of 4,976,000, 5,349,000, 5,750,000, 6,000,000 and 6,300,000 for the years 2026, 2027, 2028, 2029 and 2030, respectively.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 6,093 hospitals in the U.S., representing more than 913,000 beds and approximately 34.4 million inpatient admissions annually.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the American Hospital Association's most recent annual survey data (2023), there are approximately 6,093 hospitals in the U.S., representing more than 913,000 beds and approximately 34.4 million inpatient admissions annually.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 7,556 dialysis clinics in the United States serving more than 500,000 patients.
tamClaims.sentence: As of early 2025, there are approximately 7,556 dialysis clinics in the United States serving more than 500,000 patients.

tamClaims.claim: Installed base of hemodialysis units in the United States likely exceeds 200,000.
tamClaims.sentence: While precise counts of hemodialysis machines in use are not publicly reported on a routine national basis, industry sources indicate that the installed base in the United States likely exceeds 200,000 hemodialysis units, consistent with estimates used in market analyses.

tamClaims.claim: On any given day, approximately one in 31 hospitalized patients has at least one healthcare-associated infection.
tamClaims.sentence: According to a June 2025 report from the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), on any given day, approximately one in 31 hospitalized patients has at least one healthcare-associated infection (HAI), underscoring the scale of infection risk within hospital environments.
